common.description: Notes, tips, and real-life experience about tech in New Zealand

about.title: Latinos in NZ: getting started in tech, without the fluff
about.description: I'm Matías, a frontend developer living in New Zealand. If you're looking for your first tech job here, or just trying to understand how things work, we can have a free chat. I'll share what I learned, useful resources, and practical tips from my own experience.

moreAbout.title: A bit about my path
moreAbout.description1: I started in tech in Argentina, working as a frontend developer for different companies. After a few years, I decided to move to New Zealand and figure out how the local market worked from the inside.
moreAbout.description2: It wasn't a straight line, but it taught me a lot. I've worked with Kiwi companies both in person and remotely, and I picked up useful lessons about interviews, CVs, culture, and the day-to-day of tech work here.
moreAbout.description3: I don't have every answer, but I've been through many of these steps. If you're starting out in tech or thinking about moving to New Zealand, I can tell you what helped me and what I'd do differently.

booking.title: Let's have a chat
booking.description: Bring your questions about CVs, interviews, the local market, or your first steps in tech in New Zealand. We'll keep it simple and practical.

buyMeCoffee.title: Support my work
buyMeCoffee.description: If something here helped you, you can buy me a coffee. It helps me keep sharing notes, resources, and time with the Latin American tech community in New Zealand.

footer.description: Dev in NZ sharing what I wish I'd known when I started.
